120002.explanation: Residents must be able to participate and decide about Nijmegen. This can be through a referendum, but we prefer new and better participation forms that allow residents to take over municipal tasks and create new plans. Think of a citizens' council and the so-called right to challenge.

120005.explanation: Nijmegen is a cozy city with many nice cafés and restaurants. We should cherish that. Under current rules, the hospitality sector decides their opening hours, only between 04:00 and 08:00 must doors be closed to new guests. These rules work well, and we want them to remain so.

120009.explanation: Terraces are warmed by heat cushions, blankets, and atmosphere, not by energy-consuming heat lamps. We will stop using terrace heaters that consume unnecessary energy and support entrepreneurs in switching to sustainable alternatives.

120010.explanation: A beautiful and cozy New Year's celebration can also be done without fireworks. This keeps it safe and pleasant for everyone, including emergency workers and (house) pets. We are happy to explore beautiful and environmentally friendly alternatives such as a drone show.

120015.explanation: A local broadcaster like RN7 ensures Nijmegen residents know what is happening in our city and surroundings, from the streets to the city council. Many local media struggle to survive. Therefore, we will continue municipal support here.

120020.explanation: Safety is a joint effort, in the neighborhood, district, and at school. Enforcement tackles insecurity. We choose to tackle insecurity preventively, for example with more youth and community workers and by creating safer public spaces (such as good street lighting).

120021.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important, but camera surveillance is not a good solution. Privacy is a great asset and must not be violated lightly. Camera surveillance is therefore only used for compelling safety reasons and never longer than necessary.

120024.explanation: Nijmegen has a severe housing shortage. That is why it is important to build new homes and ensure everyone has a fair chance at housing. A starter loan helps first-time buyers with this.

120027.explanation: Houses are for living in, not for making money. If you buy a house, you must live in it yourself, the so-called owner-occupancy obligation. We also advocate for protection against buy-to-rent landlords. This prevents landlords from buying affordable homes and renting them out at excessive prices.

120030.explanation: People are not on social welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only cause more insecurity and new problems.

120032.explanation: As long as The Hague does not provide a decent minimum income, we must ensure every resident can make ends meet. Raising the income threshold (so people can qualify for poverty support sooner) ensures a larger group of Nijmegen residents can live without financial worries.

120034.explanation: Everyone has the right to a good life, which should not depend on income or financial situation. In Nijmegen, we stand for all people in our city having a decent income. A basic income can provide this; therefore, we must definitely continue experimenting with it.

120036.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

120039.explanation: Everyone must receive the care they need. But receiving care is vulnerable and intimate; therefore, we find it important that people can choose a care provider who matches their faith, culture, or beliefs. Discrimination is never allowed.

120045.explanation: We prepare existing business parks for the future. There are more than enough redevelopment opportunities in existing locations. This makes these places suitable for sustainable and circular initiatives. By investing, we also create extra jobs.

120047.explanation: People urgently looking for housing because they would otherwise be homeless should, in our view, be given priority. This can concern status holders, but more often single parents, youth leaving youth care, or homeless people.

120053.explanation: Our history, heritage, and culture make Nijmegen the rich and beautiful city it is. But for GroenLinks, this is more than just language; therefore, we choose to invest in everything that makes Nijmegen truly Nijmegen.

120055.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing. GroenLinks therefore chooses 40% social rental in new projects. In total, two-thirds of new homes in Nijmegen must be affordable.

120061.explanation: GroenLinks does not choose symptom treatment. We focus on preventing homelessness and providing the right help and support to ensure all homeless people in Nijmegen have a roof over their heads.

120062.explanation: Visitors from outside Nijmegen are encouraged to park at park-and-ride locations with good connections. This way, people can easily and affordably get into the city, and we can green and beautify the center. People with mobility impairments can, of course, still park in the center.

120063.explanation: Emissions from non-electric mopeds and scooters significantly impact the health of cyclists and pedestrians nearby. Therefore, we phase out polluting scooters, and with scrappage and replacement subsidies, we ensure everyone can afford an electric scooter.

120064.explanation: The streets in our city are getting busier, so we must carefully consider how we use the limited space. GroenLinks chooses space for greenery, social encounters, cycling, walking, and public transport over more space for passenger cars.

120065.explanation: Emissions from wood burning are unhealthy; therefore, wood burning must be banned during red and orange air quality alerts. Moreover, we continue the scrappage subsidy for existing wood stoves, and no new wood stoves will be allowed.

120066.explanation: Splitting existing large houses is an ideal response to the high demand for smaller homes. The municipality will actively support this. Additionally, we also focus on splits that create new shared housing groups and housing associations.

120067.explanation: Everyone has the right to a safe workplace, including sex workers. Therefore, we keep the designated street prostitution zone open. This maintains a safe place for street sex workers, with security, to perform their work and a lounge where they can easily access care, support, and each other.

120069.explanation: The Four Days Marches Festival is the biggest and most beautiful free festival in the Netherlands. Cafés and festival organizers cover the costs for stages and artists with profits from drinks. Supermarkets do not contribute through their sales, and without intervention, the festival cannot remain free.

120070.explanation: Culture House Lindenberg is the place where every Nijmegen resident can experience culture. To ensure this in the future, the current building is too outdated. That is why we will renew the building as sustainably as possible with room for social organizations. For us, 'generous' means sufficient funding.

120002.explanation: We make Nijmegen together! We involve residents through neighborhood conversations and a citizens' council. For major city decisions, a referendum will help to honestly reveal public opinion and build support. This way, Nijmegen residents get real influence.

120005.explanation: We value freedom for entrepreneurs. Spreading out closing times can also help reduce nuisance during nighttime hours. However, entrepreneurs and the municipality must together safeguard the limits regarding livability for local residents.

120009.explanation: We favor education, not senseless bans. We want to waste less energy, but a ban is not our way. We prefer to consult with entrepreneurs so they can come up with alternatives themselves while continuing to operate well and customer-friendly.

120010.explanation: A central fireworks show can be a good alternative if a fireworks ban really works. As long as fireworks are still for sale and possession is not banned, enforcement remains a problem. Only when this is properly regulated will there be capacity for police deployment and a safe show.

120015.explanation: Local media and neighborhood papers are indispensable for a strong local democracy. They provide reliable reporting, made for and by Nijmegen residents. Therefore, we support local broadcasters and neighborhood papers so they can report on what is happening in the city and districts.

120020.explanation: The City Party takes safety seriously 24/7. A safe city requires visible enforcement, both in the city center and neighborhoods. That is why we deploy more BOAs (public order officers) and expand that capacity. With more enforcement officers on the street, we address nuisance faster and ensure peace and safety.

120021.explanation: The City Party takes safety seriously 24/7. Everyone must feel safe in Nijmegen. Camera surveillance helps tackle nuisance and increases the feeling of safety. Therefore, we use cameras targetedly, as part of a broad and smart safety approach.

120024.explanation: Starters have difficulty in the housing market. In Nijmegen, we already have a starter's loan. It helps people who are buying a house for the first time to just bridge that final piece and continue living in our beautiful city.

120027.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in. That is why Nijmegen already has an owner-occupancy requirement and an anti-speculation clause. This way, we prevent houses from being bought up for profit and keep homes available and affordable for residents.

120030.explanation: People on benefits should primarily be led as quickly as possible towards independent living. We focus on guidance programs that truly deliver results. No unnecessary busywork therapy, but rather a thorough program toward greater financial security.

120032.explanation: Poverty does not belong in Nijmegen. Many Nijmegen residents fall just outside current rules, while struggling to make ends meet. By raising the income threshold, we strengthen financial security and prevent small problems from escalating into debt and poverty.

120034.explanation: We choose for financial security, trust, and fewer rules. A basic income can only be implemented nationally. Previous trials have not been successful. That is why Nijmegen will introduce a Social Participation Budget, giving more opportunities for an independent life without money worries.

120036.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but 30 km/h only works if streets are actually adjusted. Just replacing a sign is a symbolic measure. Main roads must keep flowing. In residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h can fit if it improves safety and streets are designed more safely.

120039.explanation: Care must remain accessible and affordable for everyone. We consider people's backgrounds and wishes, but cannot promise that everything is always possible. Good care is about quality, availability, and respect, within what is practical and feasible.

120045.explanation: Nijmegen needs space for work and business activity. By expanding and using industrial areas more intelligently, we strengthen innovation and employment. We do this purposefully, including at De Grift, TPN-West, and the Novio Tech Campus (Winkelsteeg), with attention to sustainability and surroundings.

120047.explanation: This is not a free choice of Nijmegen, but a national obligation. Permit holders must be housed. We do this because we have to, but we prefer more support from The Hague to solve the housing shortage, so that building for everyone gets priority.

120049.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce in Nijmegen. We want to handle it carefully. We preferably place solar panels on roofs, industrial sites, and other logical places.

120053.explanation: The Nijmegen dialect is part of our heritage. And heritage connects and nurtures the feeling of belonging to this city. That is why it is important that we keep the Nijmegen dialect and other local folk culture alive. Every Nijmegen child should also know our city song 'Al Mot Ik Krupe'.

120055.explanation: In Nijmegen, we study every two years what type of housing is most needed. This shows that 30% of new homes must be social rental. There is also great demand for middle-rent and affordable owner-occupied housing. We ensure a mix in new construction that matches the city's needs.

120061.explanation: The City Party wants to make homelessness in Nijmegen unnecessary. No one should have to sleep outside. With shelter, guidance, and care, we help people to their own place. Everyone entitled to it gets a roof over their head, so that shelter becomes unnecessary in the long term.

120062.explanation: An accessible inner city is crucial for shops, hospitality, and residents. That is why we provide space for short-term parking around the center. At the edges of the inner city, such as under the Wedren, we will ensure sufficient parking capacity when Marienburg closes and keep the city economically vital.

120063.explanation: We voted against the moped ban. Because an electric moped is expensive. This ban mainly affects people with a low income. We do not anticipate national rules. First, efforts must be made to make electric mopeds affordable and to provide good charging points.

120064.explanation: Our main roads must keep flowing. The Keizer Karelplein and Oranjesingel may regain their former allure. But we will do this without squeezing car space here. We make these routes more attractive by adding trees, greenery, and flowers at the edges.

120065.explanation: Wood smoke can cause nuisance, but a ban during orange or red air quality codes is not enforceable and mainly works on paper. We choose clean air through education and conscious burning, and mediation in neighborhoods when there is nuisance. No senseless bans, but measures that really work.

120066.explanation: Splitting homes sounds like a quick solution, but often leads to nuisance, too small rooms, and extra pressure on neighborhoods in Nijmegen. That is why the rules have been tightened. Building is necessary, but not at the expense of livability and safety in neighborhoods.

120067.explanation: Closing the red-light zone solves nothing. Without supervision, sex workers disappear into illegality, with more insecurity and exploitation. At the same time, the station area is changing strongly due to housing construction. That is why we opt for provisional preservation and work towards a careful relocation in the long term.

120069.explanation: During the Four Days Marches, hospitality and events support the celebrations and invest a lot. They also contribute to keeping the city clean and safe. Supermarkets benefit as well but do not share this responsibility. That is unfair and not in the interest of the city and the Four Days (celebrations).

120070.explanation: The Lindenberg is important for culture in the city. The building is our property but is outdated and poorly accessible. We want to improve the location and investigate whether staying or relocating is better. Affordability is paramount: renovation yes, lavish investment no.

120002.explanation: Democracy becomes stronger when people can not only vote but also participate, think along, and co-decide. Therefore, we are open to referenda and want to use citizens' forums, digital platforms, and a youth council more often.

120005.explanation: Cafés and restaurants should set their own opening hours, always with attention to neighborhood livability. Entrepreneurs know what works, while the municipality cooperates with residents to monitor nuisance.

120009.explanation: A ban on terrace heating has little effect and unnecessarily limits entrepreneurs. D66 prefers effective ways to save energy and make things more sustainable, such as insulation and installing a heat network.

120010.explanation: A joint, professional New Year's Eve fireworks show provides a festive moment for the whole city and prevents unacceptable nuisance, pollution, and damage caused by private fireworks nowadays.

120015.explanation: Good (investigative) journalism checks power, explains what is happening in politics, and brings decisions closer to the people. It is important that local media remain strong.

120020.explanation: There is currently a structural shortage, causing enforcement to lag behind. Extra BOAs are needed to enforce rules and keep the city safe and livable. Additionally, we want BOAs to actively address street intimidation.

120021.explanation: D66 is cautious with camera surveillance. It may only be used temporarily for specific problems, such as street intimidation or demonstrable insecurity; structurally, we choose other solutions like more enforcement and better street lighting.

120024.explanation: Starters currently have extra difficulty in the housing market and deserve support to buy a first home. Starter loans can help enter the housing market, alongside our efforts to build more affordable homes.

120027.explanation: We consider an owner-occupancy requirement important to prevent affordable owner-occupied homes from being bought up for speculation and to give starters a fair chance. At the same time, we acknowledge that renting is also necessary for people who (yet) cannot or do not want to buy.

120030.explanation: D66 finds it reasonable that people on social assistance do volunteer work, as long as it is appropriate and considers an individual's personal situation.

120032.explanation: We strive for a simpler and fairer poverty system. No tangle of schemes, but a supplement to the income to a sufficient-not lavish but adequate-level. The current income threshold ensures that people can fully participate in our city.

120034.explanation: The costs of a municipal basic income are too high, and a municipality cannot afford or raise it via taxes. However, we are open to national trials, where the government arranges costs and conditions.

120036.explanation: D66 Nijmegen does not choose a general 30 km/h limit throughout the entire built-up area. We want lower speeds where it improves safety and livability, but also keep the city accessible by leaving space for good flow on main roads.

120039.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is always important. People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them and where they feel comfortable and safe. This freedom is essential for good, people-centered care.

120045.explanation: Space in and around the city is scarce. Therefore, we focus on densification and smarter use of existing industrial areas to create more space for businesses and jobs without affecting additional open spaces.

120047.explanation: D66 stands for a fair and balanced allocation of housing. Refugees with a residence permit should be able to start their life in Nijmegen as quickly as possible. That is why we support the current arrangement, in which priority is not exclusive to any one group.

120049.explanation: Solar panels are urgently needed for the energy transition. Therefore, D66 does not want to prohibit solar panels on agricultural land.

120053.explanation: Schools decide independently on the content of education. The municipality should not impose obligations regarding this.

120055.explanation: We target a balanced mix of social rental, mid-priced, and private sector housing per neighborhood, fitting the housing needs and with attention to flow-through. We do not opt for a fixed percentage across the entire city.

120061.explanation: Shelters for homeless and roofless people remain necessary, also in the city center. We choose well-regulated shelters with guidance and actively address nuisance.

120062.explanation: We focus on parking in garages and Park & Ride facilities to create more space for greenery, walking, and cycling. If a shortage of parking spaces occurs, we will expand the spots in garages.

120063.explanation: Air quality in Nijmegen is under pressure and does not yet meet WHO standards. The ban on polluting mopeds and scooters helps improve this and must therefore remain.

120064.explanation: Narrowing roads is not a goal in itself. Our city must remain accessible for pedestrians, cyclists, cars, and public transport. We monitor this closely.

120065.explanation: Air quality in Nijmegen is under pressure and does not yet meet WHO standards. Temporarily banning wood burning during orange and red codes yields immediate health benefits and less nuisance.

120066.explanation: By splitting existing homes, many more people can live in the city without using extra space. This helps quickly and concretely in tackling the housing shortage.

120067.explanation: This is necessary to better protect women and provide them with targeted care, guidance, and perspective. Closure is not ignoring the problem, but a step towards more safety and support.

120069.explanation: We find it important that visitors during the Four Days Marches contribute to the program and the city. The current regulation helps keep the atmosphere manageable and safe and ensures that costs are fairly distributed.

120070.explanation: Culture is essential for a vibrant city. De Lindenberg is an important cultural facility and deserves a solid investment so that the Culture House can grow along with a larger and more diverse Nijmegen.

120002.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

120005.explanation: More freedom for hospitality businesses can come at the expense of livability in residential neighborhoods. Municipal frameworks help maintain a balance between liveliness and peace for residents, especially in the evenings and at night. The current opening hours work well and do not need to be adjusted.

120009.explanation: Terrace heating is nice but consumes a lot of energy and is bad for the climate. Simply banning it is not the right measure. The municipality should work together with entrepreneurs to explore how it can be done differently and better. This is how support for changes is created.

120010.explanation: The best New Year’s celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one big fireworks show organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows everyone to enjoy a cozy New Year’s Eve. We also look at sustainable alternatives, such as a show with lasers and/or drones.

120015.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to keep afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

120020.explanation: A livable city is a safe city. Therefore, we must strongly focus on prevention. That means there must be enough bylaw enforcement officers as well as enough police officers.

120021.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras do not contribute to that. Moreover, privacy is a great good that should not be impaired lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcers on the street who can also intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

120024.explanation: Starters have a hard time, so we help them with a loan. This gives them a better chance to buy a house. In addition, we continue to ensure that homes remain affordable so that this extra support does not cause house prices to rise further.

120027.explanation: Houses are for living in, not for making money. In Nijmegen, you must therefore live in the house you buy yourself. That is why we have introduced the owner-occupancy obligation in Nijmegen.

120030.explanation: Policy based on trust, guidance, and development yields more sustainable results than obligations. People progress more with support, education, and customization than with sanctions.

120032.explanation: We want a new minimum subsistence level. That means everyone in Nijmegen should have enough money to get by. If that is not possible, the municipality helps with an income supplement. We want a broad approach that looks at each person individually. We base this on trust.

120034.explanation: Everyone has the right to a secure existence. In Nijmegen, we already work with a broad people-centered approach in which benefits and low incomes are supplemented when necessary. We prefer to allocate all resources and focus to this rather than on a basic income trial.

120036.explanation: The figures clearly show: with a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this makes a world of difference. Furthermore, the streets are quieter and the air cleaner.

120039.explanation: We must ensure that care fits the individual. Therefore, we believe we should offer customized care where possible.

120045.explanation: A thriving city is a city with businesses and sufficient employment, also for practically trained people. When expanding industrial areas, we must always look critically at where that is possible and it must not be at the expense of housing or clean air.

120047.explanation: Certain housing seekers in Nijmegen must be given priority. Refugees with a residence permit are one of the priority groups. In the vast majority of cases, priority concerns people who risk becoming homeless after a divorce, starters under 27 years old, or elderly people who are moving along.

120049.explanation: Nijmegen is growing. More people come to live and work here, so more energy is needed. A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy.

120053.explanation: Nimweegs belongs to our city. Nijmegen already invests heavily in local, cultural, historical, and other initiatives so that young residents also learn the Nimweeg traditions. Primary school classes focus on the Dutch language and basic skills.

120055.explanation: In Nijmegen, you must be able to live affordably. We choose to build at least 40% social rental housing. In addition, we choose enough mid-priced homes, student housing, and affordable owner-occupied homes. This creates resilient and mixed neighborhoods where you can live comfortably.

120061.explanation: Homelessness requires care and support and more housing. Relocating shelters does not solve homelessness but shifts the problem to other neighborhoods. The municipality must actively tackle nuisance with residents and entrepreneurs.

120062.explanation: The center must be easily accessible for everyone. We focus on good transfer points with fast and cheap bus connections and shared mobility. But not everyone can walk, cycle, or use public transport. The center must also remain accessible by car for those who need it.

120063.explanation: The ban on polluting scooters was introduced to make the city cleaner and healthier. At the same time, we see that not everyone can simply afford an electric scooter. That is why we choose support: whoever hands in their polluting scooter receives a subsidy for a cleaner model.

120064.explanation: Narrowed roads can lead to congestion and worse accessibility for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs, especially during peak times. Targeted measures, smart traffic control, and safe cycling infrastructure work better than general narrowing of roads.

120065.explanation: Good air quality is important. In Nijmegen, we already follow the burning guide which advises against burning during orange or red codes, but we cannot enforce this locally.

120066.explanation: Splitting homes can relatively quickly provide extra living space without the need for new construction. This helps starters, students, and singles who currently find it difficult to find a home.

120067.explanation: The tippelzone provides supervision, assistance, and a safe working environment. Closure increases the chance of violence, exploitation, and unsafe situations. It causes a waterbed effect. Prostitution does not disappear but moves to streets and neighborhoods where supervision is lacking.

120069.explanation: The Four Days Marches festivals are the largest and most beautiful free event in the Netherlands. The Four Days Marches Foundation and many entrepreneurs in the city invest in the festivals. Supermarkets in the center do not contribute to this. Supermarkets outside the center may continue to sell alcohol.

120070.explanation: De Lindenberg is an important place for cultural education, participation, and amateur arts. The municipality must contribute to new construction or renovation but only once a good and sound plan with a clear price tag is presented and approved by the city council.

120002.explanation: The advisory referendum and citizen assemblies will be given a role in important decisions, such as major construction projects or municipal reorganizations. We will also enable neighborhood referenda.

120005.explanation: We want to guard against promoting a 24-hour economy. Rest is important for everyone and we must prevent nuisance to residents. Small entrepreneurs often lose out.

120009.explanation: Terrace heating is discouraged. Together with local entrepreneurs, we tackle energy waste and promote sustainable alternatives.

120010.explanation: Festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers, can take place as long as people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer any harm.

120015.explanation: Strong, independent local media and regional broadcasters such as RN7 and the various neighborhood newspapers are very important for the residents of Nijmegen. There will be no budget cuts. To promote independent (investigative) journalism, a widely accessible local media fund will be established.

120020.explanation: There is currently insufficient enforcement against fireworks and poachers. Women and LGBTIQ+ people want safe streets.

120021.explanation: Municipal camera surveillance is handled very cautiously due to privacy considerations. It may only be temporary and under strict conditions, such as a maximum retention period for the footage.

120024.explanation: Sufficient truly affordable homes must be built and people must be fairly rewarded for their work. Then such a starter loan is unnecessary. As long as that is not yet the case, a starter loan can help.

120027.explanation: Measures will be taken against speculation with housing and vacancy, such as an owner-occupancy requirement and a vacancy ordinance with sanctions. Long-term vacant properties will be made habitable.

120030.explanation: The municipality supports benefit recipients to do volunteer work, informal care, and internships, but it will not be obligatory.

120032.explanation: Nijmegen has already increased this and maintains the current minimum policy for incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. This includes, for example, special assistance and collective health insurance.

120034.explanation: The introduction of a basic income creates more equality. The municipality will indicate to the national government, which is responsible for this, that Nijmegen wants to conduct another pilot with a basic income that reflects current insights.

120036.explanation: As planned, there will be a maximum speed of 30 km/h for motorized traffic within built-up areas. Where possible, we will expand this further. Road designs will be adapted where possible, which in turn creates room for greening.

120039.explanation: Care must be aligned with the person behind the care request. Nijmegen chooses tailored care, with respect for individual identity, religion, or cultural background, so that care is accessible and effective. Quality, equality, and non-discrimination will always remain the foundation.

120045.explanation: Business parks that no longer have a future or cause too much nuisance to surrounding residential areas will be made circular and clean or transformed into residential neighborhoods and green spaces. In restructuring business parks with future prospects, space will be used much more efficiently.

120047.explanation: The municipality meets the target for housing status holders and speaks out against the proposed law that removes priority for status holders for social rental housing. If the law is unexpectedly passed, Nijmegen will create a local priority scheme for status holders.

120049.explanation: Agricultural land is not intended for solar parks but for plant-based food production and nature development and, if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing.

120053.explanation: Basically not against paying attention to the Nijmegen dialect, but the municipality does not control the primary school curriculum.

120055.explanation: For us, the minimum threshold is 40%.

120061.explanation: The city center remains the place where homeless people gather. If shelters disappear from the center, this has a counterproductive effect.

120062.explanation: We want less space for cars in residential neighborhoods and more space for greenery, safety, and livability. New street parking encourages car use and harms livability.

120063.explanation: Clean air and health come first. Expanding the environmental zone is necessary to reduce emissions and protect residents; reversing the ban on non-electric mopeds and scooters does not fit with this.

120064.explanation: Main roads in the municipality, such as IJpenbroekweg, Oranjesingel, and Keizer Karelplein, will be narrowed to reduce traffic speed and add more greenery. Narrowing and other measures will not harm bicycle and pedestrian traffic.

120065.explanation: Nijmegen will completely ban wood burning, both indoors and outdoors. Wood smoke causes enormous fine particulate pollution and health problems for many residents. Nijmegen will maintain the complaint point for smoke nuisance and continue informing residents about the disadvantages of wood burning.

120066.explanation: Converting rooms and/or splitting houses can sometimes be a solution for the shortage of suitable homes. At the neighborhood level, it must be assessed whether this fits the surroundings, and it must not come at the expense of public space. Splitting must comply with rules to prevent abuses.

120067.explanation: We choose safety, health, and the rights of sex workers. Closing or banning sex work increases insecurity and exploitation; good regulation and support work better.

120069.explanation: The Four Days Marches Festivals must remain affordable. Therefore, we allow temporary supermarket sales so visitors are not dependent on expensive sales and a regulated channel with age verification remains. However, we attach strict conditions: no advertising and strict enforcement.

120070.explanation: Only co-pay for renovation. From the perspective of the circular city, renovation and reuse of existing buildings is the starting point, even if it costs more than new construction.

120002.explanation: Referenda are democracy in action, in the view of the SP, and therefore of great added value.

120005.explanation: For livability, we support regulating opening hours. These do not need to change, according to the SP.

120009.explanation: We support a discount on terrace permit fees for entrepreneurs who forego terrace heating.

120010.explanation: Now that fireworks are banned, this seems like a nice alternative to us. Other, more sustainable alternatives are also possible.

120015.explanation: Local democracy benefits from good and critical reporting.

120020.explanation: The SP wants to improve safety and supervision in the neighborhoods. We advocate deploying 100 city jobs to increase supervision.

120021.explanation: Camera surveillance will, according to the SP, only be used where it is proportionate and effective. More camera surveillance is unnecessary and an infringement on privacy, according to the SP.

120024.explanation: It is increasingly difficult for starters to get a mortgage. A starter loan helps them with that and also ensures more rental homes become available (flow-through).

120027.explanation: The owner-occupancy requirement and anti-speculation clause remain in force in Nijmegen. This prevents speculation by slumlords. We also support a vacancy ordinance.

120030.explanation: If we have work for social assistance recipients, they must receive a full salary for it. Working while keeping the benefit is exploitation.

120032.explanation: We increase the target group using Nijmegen's participation scheme to 150% of the social minimum and expand minimum arrangements for sports, culture, and education. This way, we also reach the working class who can barely make ends meet.

120034.explanation: A basic income of sufficient level is not feasible locally. People with little money would actually be worse off because we cannot compensate them. We prefer thorough redistribution of wealth and income on a national level, and control over the huge prices of food.

120036.explanation: Traffic must be safer. That is why we support lowering the maximum speed limit within the city from 50 to 30 kilometers per hour as much as possible. This promotes traffic safety. This measure must not be at the expense of bus transport.

120039.explanation: As a municipality, we encourage Nijmegen organizations to work in a culturally sensitive manner and to reflect the diversity of our city.

120045.explanation: This mainly depends on the type of companies. Polluting companies, companies that treat their staff poorly or invest in weapons and genocide are not welcome according to the SP. Sustainable, socially responsible companies with employee participation are.

120047.explanation: Refugees have fled war and do not have a social network in the Netherlands. That is why we support giving them priority. Incidentally, this concerns only about 6% of social rental homes.

120049.explanation: The SP opposes solar farms because they come at the expense of green spaces and biodiversity.

120053.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of the schools themselves; the municipality can support if needed. It should not lead to extra workload for teachers.

120055.explanation: 35% is still far too little. If it were up to the SP, in the coming period we will build 75% social rental housing. That way we tackle the housing shortage and catch up on backlogs. More money will go to the Housing Fund to finance these homes.

120061.explanation: Moving the shelters does not eliminate the nuisance. Homeless people also come to the center without shelter because that is where facilities and people are. However, according to the SP, more shelter places should be added elsewhere to relieve pressure from the city center.

120062.explanation: We support keeping the parking spaces for residents of the courtyards and alleys in the city center.

120063.explanation: Electric mopeds and scooters are still too expensive for people with low income, while these people contribute relatively little to CO2 emissions. Therefore, a ban is unjust. However, we should discourage non-electric mopeds and scooters.

120064.explanation: Important access roads such as the boulevards should not be narrowed until public transport has been sufficiently improved, according to the SP.

120065.explanation: Wood burning is prohibited at yellow, orange, and red codes in the Smoker’s Guide. This supports people with respiratory complaints and keeps our air clean. The Smoker’s Guide will be updated to make it more reliable. For an equal and solidary city, we take each other into account.

120066.explanation: We support splitting homes by housing corporations to meet the need for smaller homes. According to the SP, it should not be used by developers as a way to circumvent the rules on multiple occupancy.

120067.explanation: We will not close the tippelzone without an alternative for the care currently offered there.

120069.explanation: For people with low incomes, the Four Days Marches Festivals are becoming increasingly inaccessible due to rising prices. The municipality claims this ban is to combat alcohol abuse, but meanwhile, the entire revenue model of the festivals is based on selling as much alcohol as possible.

120070.explanation: We support the current funding for the renovation of the Lindenberg but are not in favor of additional money. According to the SP, that should go to small cultural organizations, societies, and hotspots. A second Valkhof drama must be prevented.

120002.explanation: In a referendum, mainly opponents of an issue vote. Therefore, Nijmegen's VVD wants the municipal council to vote on important issues and make a balanced consideration of all interests.

120005.explanation: The owners of cafes and restaurants should decide themselves when their establishments close. This also prevents nuisance if all pubs closed at the same time.

120009.explanation: A winter terrace is a nice addition to Nijmegen’s hospitality offering.

120010.explanation: In cooperation with local (hospitality) entrepreneurs, the municipality should explore whether a fireworks show is possible.

120015.explanation: Nijmegen’s VVD considers the local broadcaster important but does not choose to allocate extra funds.

120020.explanation: The (feeling of) insecurity in Nijmegen is too high. This can improve through more enforcement officers, stricter measures, and more control.

120021.explanation: The (feeling of) insecurity in Nijmegen is too high. Camera surveillance ensures better enforcement and control and also works preventively.

120024.explanation: The municipality is not a bank.

120027.explanation: A healthy housing market also requires sufficient rental supply.

120030.explanation: People who receive social assistance benefits must participate in society as much as necessary.

120032.explanation: People who need help must receive it. But currently, the income difference between benefits and work is too small.

120034.explanation: Everyone should have a good income, preferably from work.

120036.explanation: There are roads where 30 km/h is a fine speed. But there are also roads where it is perfectly safe and appropriate to drive faster than 30 km/h.

120039.explanation: Everyone has the right to good care from a good healthcare provider.

120045.explanation: There is still sufficient room for expansion.

120047.explanation: Nijmegen’s VVD opposes a target group policy for allocating social rental homes. Only people with urgent need receive priority.

120049.explanation: But Nijmegen has no agricultural land.

120053.explanation: The municipality should interfere as little as possible with the schools’ curriculum.

120061.explanation: There is currently too much nuisance from the MFC at the current location. This must stop.

120062.explanation: The city center must be welcoming to all visitors, including those who come by car.

120064.explanation: The roads around the city center are vital for traffic flow. Narrowing, for example, the Keizer Karelplein is a really bizarre idea. The city must be accessible to everyone, also by car.

120065.explanation: This ban is not enforceable. The municipality is better off focusing on good information about proper wood burning.

120066.explanation: This can contribute to reducing the housing shortage.

120067.explanation: The tippelzone is a safe place for sex workers, where care and assistance are also offered. Closing it spreads the nuisance and makes providing care more difficult.

120069.explanation: The hospitality entrepreneurs make the festival in the city center possible through the provision of stages and artists. This is financed by alcohol sales. Allowing supermarkets to sell alcohol without contributing to the festival's costs makes organizing the festival impossible.

120070.explanation: It is important that the Lindenberg continues to exist, either through new construction or renovation. A conversation about this needs to be initiated.

120002.explanation: Many municipal issues are too complex for a simple yes/no question. Therefore, the CDA does not consider referendums a good instrument to ask the residents of Nijmegen for their opinion.

120005.explanation: In Nijmegen, cafes and restaurants are already allowed to determine their own opening hours. The CDA wants to keep it that way. The only restriction is that after 4:00 a.m., no one may be admitted into a bar, but the establishment does not have to close. The CDA wants to maintain this regulation method to limit nuisance.

120009.explanation: There are also energy-efficient ways to heat terraces or seating areas, for which residents of Nijmegen have found good solutions.

120010.explanation: The municipality already organizes a fireworks show during the Four Days Marches. Especially when people are asked to use less personal fireworks, they should be assured of a municipal show.

120015.explanation: The local broadcaster is important and must be able to rely on funding. The CDA wants to continue offering that certainty to the local broadcaster. This can also be achieved in ways other than additional money. We must also prevent a dependency relationship between local media and politics. National funding prevents that.

120020.explanation: The CDA believes there should be more BOAs (Public Enforcement Officers) who can ensure that everyone can walk safely in the streets.

120021.explanation: Nijmegen has certain places in the city that feel unsafe for women, the LGBTIQ+ community, or due to incidents. The CDA considers it important that residents themselves indicate where they want cameras, considering privacy and the fact that residents themselves know which areas are unsafe.

120024.explanation: Young people in Nijmegen especially struggle to find housing. The CDA wants to offer young people better opportunities with a starter loan.

120027.explanation: The CDA finds it important to protect houses from investors. Especially when affordable homes are put on the market, these should not be taken over by investors.

120030.explanation: The CDA is convinced that participating in society, for example through volunteer work, is essential for people's self-esteem: the feeling of belonging. The municipality must work with participation coaches to look for a good solution together with people on social assistance.

120032.explanation: The municipality must do more to prevent income loss. This loss occurs when people (start to) work more. The CDA has made a start here by instructing the municipal council to commission a new poverty system.

120034.explanation: A basic income is impossible to arrange at the municipal level. It simply costs too much money for the municipality. This should be implemented by the national government.

120036.explanation: The CDA ensures more traffic safety in Nijmegen and more safe bike paths and crossings. Within residential neighborhoods, the speed limit must be reduced to 30 km/h, but on main roads, there must also be flow for buses, ambulances, fire departments, and police.

120039.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people may choose their own healthcare provider, as long as it complies with all laws and regulations.

120045.explanation: Nijmegen lacks space, also for businesses. The CDA finds it important that money is also earned in the city and surrounding area. Therefore, the municipality must enable expansion.

120047.explanation: There is already urgency in Nijmegen regarding status holders. Stopping priority housing for refugees with residence permits is pointless as long as there are no alternative homes. Refugees must have a roof over their heads somewhere.

120049.explanation: For the CDA, such a ban makes little sense, since Nijmegen has almost no agricultural land.

120053.explanation: 't Nimweegs is a wonderful piece of heritage. As a city, we must cherish it. It is important that children learn more about 't Nimweegs at school as part of our culture.

120055.explanation: The CDA believes it is essential that there are enough affordable homes for people in the city. At the same time, we must ensure there are also enough owner-occupied homes to move out of social rental housing.

120061.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people without a roof over their heads or a home can be received well and humanely. The homeless shelter could be better distributed, in the CDA’s opinion. The current homeless shelter has a magnet effect and causes a lot of nuisance. The CDA wants to investigate whether the homeless shelter

120062.explanation: The municipality is removing more and more parking spaces from the Inner City and Lower City and closing more parking garages. This makes the center inaccessible for people with mobility issues: the CDA therefore wants to retain more parking spaces. A good pedestrian area also requires good parking facilities.

120063.explanation: The municipality has chosen the ban. People have made investments, often with municipal support, to buy new electric scooters. Undoing this would, according to the CDA, be a waste of the spending of Nijmegen residents.

120064.explanation: Essential roads around the center lead to Keizer Karelplein and the Waal Bridge. Narrowing roads is therefore impossible in Nijmegen, according to the CDA. However, the CDA wants to strengthen public transport and add park-and-ride locations to relieve traffic around the center.

120065.explanation: The CDA believes that wood burning should be prohibited during code red or unfavorable weather due to nuisance and health reasons.

120066.explanation: More and more single young people or elderly people live in single-family homes in Nijmegen. As long as it does not harm the livability of the neighborhood, the municipality should make dividing and splitting homes easier.

120067.explanation: The CDA thinks it is better for the tippelzone (red-light area) if it is closed and replaced with a strong care approach for the women working there.

120069.explanation: Alcohol sales during the Four Days Marches are an important part of funding the festivities, while alcohol from supermarkets causes litter problems. The CDA wants to keep the festivities enjoyable for everyone and therefore supports this measure.

120070.explanation: The Lindenberg is an important cultural center used by many Nijmegen residents. The CDA does find it important that the municipality strictly monitors that budgets are honest and costs do not become unnecessarily high.

120002.explanation: Citizen participation is a very important theme for the Pirate Party! We support citizens' assemblies and an elected mayor.

120005.explanation: As long as it is coordinated in good consultation with the neighborhood residents.

120009.explanation: We do not like prohibitions. But it is desirable to use energy smarter and more efficiently. For example, heated patio cushions.

120010.explanation: As a society, we must do everything to prevent injuries caused by fireworks in Nijmegen. The municipality could organize local activities in cooperation with neighborhood associations and/or businesses that strengthen neighborhood cohesion.

120015.explanation: Yes, additional funding may be necessary, but only under strict conditions of independence, transparency, and citizen involvement. The goal is not a “municipal mouthpiece,” but a free platform for residents.

120020.explanation: More police is fine, but rather no BOAs. They do not have sufficient powers to really make a difference.

120021.explanation: Research shows that the presence of such systems has little effect on the extent of crime and, at best, the crime simply relocates to another place.

120024.explanation: If the private market does not dare to take this up, it is good if the municipality sets up a fund for this.

120027.explanation: Nijmegen should not build for speculators but for its own residents!

120030.explanation: Motivating people to contribute is fine. Feeling useful is important for your health. But obliging people to work goes too far.

120032.explanation: There should be no income limit but more customization. We are in favor of an Unconditional Basic Income.

120034.explanation: The Pirate Party supports an Unconditional Basic Income.

120036.explanation: Reducing the entire built-up area to 30 km/h goes too far for us. However, in certain neighborhoods such as Bottendaal, Brakkenstein, and the Professoren buurt, it can be considered whether the speed can be reduced to 30 km/h. We need to listen to the residents of these neighborhoods.

120039.explanation: If these are available, that should not be a problem. We believe that understanding each other will also improve care.

120045.explanation: Existing sites should not be expanded but arranged more efficiently. Additionally, the hardening of the landscape must be countered.

120047.explanation: There must be flow-through in the asylum seekers' centers (AZCs). Otherwise, they become overcrowded and cause problems. We must adhere to the agreements made at the national level as long as the dispersion law is not withdrawn and the policy remains unchanged.

120049.explanation: There are sufficient alternatives to install solar panels on existing buildings. Expensive agricultural land should not be sacrificed for this.

120053.explanation: If children want to progress in this society, focusing on math and language is more important. This increases equal opportunities for all children.

120055.explanation: An affordable home should be available for every resident. Social rental homes are preferably built by housing corporations or through Collective Private Commissioning.

120061.explanation: Shifting the problem makes no sense. It's better to look for a good solution.

120062.explanation: We do need to keep an eye on the increase in the number of car owners. In addition, we are heavily investing in shared cars.

120063.explanation: In other cities, this measure has led to significant improvements in traffic safety, flow, air quality, and noise nuisance. Everyone should be clearly informed about this.

120064.explanation: Cyclists must have enough space to move around in the city center.

120065.explanation: The Pirate Party is not in favor of symbolic legislation. Everyone knows that a municipal regulation is unenforceable. However, we do focus on good information and the replacement of old stoves. We see German legislation as a good example.

120066.explanation: A significant portion of households in Nijmegen are single-person; over 40% of residents in owner-occupied or rental homes live alone. We welcome the current initiatives of housing corporations to split homes.

120067.explanation: Policy should primarily focus on regulating street prostitution to ensure both the safety of sex workers and public order in the city.

120069.explanation: People with limited means should also be able to buy a drink. However, financial contributions for the Four Days Marches could be discussed in consultation with supermarkets.

120070.explanation: It serves as an important place for cultural participation and personal development in the city. The accessibility of the building for all Nijmegen citizens must be guaranteed.

120002.explanation: Residents should truly have a say in major decisions. Too often plans are finalized before people can participate. Referendums strengthen trust and ensure support.

120005.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the space to decide for themselves what works. Naturally, with respect for the neighborhood and livability. Less patronizing, more trust and customization.

120009.explanation: We leave that to the entrepreneur in question; the municipality should be flexible about this. Entrepreneurs are already having a hard time these days.

120010.explanation: If citizens have a need for this, then yes.

120015.explanation: Public information is important, but every euro must demonstrably benefit residents. Transparency and social value are leading.

120020.explanation: Visible enforcement increases the feeling of safety. Enforcement officers (BOAs) are approachable and close by. Safety starts in the neighborhood.

120021.explanation: Cameras can help but are not a miracle solution. Use them only where it demonstrably works. Privacy remains important.

120024.explanation: Young people hardly get a chance on the housing market anymore. A starter loan helps them get started. Starters must be residents of Nijmegen.

120027.explanation: Homes are meant for living, not for speculation. Investors displace starters and families. This must stop.

120030.explanation: Participation is important, but coercion has the opposite effect. Support and guidance must come first.

120032.explanation: Many people just fall outside the arrangements. That is unfair. Help must fit the reality.

120034.explanation: We must dare to innovate. Less control and more trust can help people move forward. A pilot provides valuable insights. People must then not become passive but fully participate.

120036.explanation: Safety is important, but not every street is the same. We choose customization. No symbolic policies.

120039.explanation: Good care connects to the individual. Freedom of choice increases trust and effectiveness.

120045.explanation: Employment is important, but not at the expense of the living environment. Expansion only where truly necessary and feasible.

120047.explanation: We cannot avoid legal obligations; we must give priority to our youth so they can build a future.

120049.explanation: Rooftops have priority, but sometimes customization is needed. No rigid ban.

120053.explanation: Local culture connects and provides identity. The Nijmegen dialect belongs to the city.

120055.explanation: The housing market is out of balance. More social rental housing is necessary. Housing is a right. But also proper homes so young people can start families.

120061.explanation: The city center is not the right place for intensive shelter facilities. Shelters must offer peace, safety, and space for good guidance. Spreading these facilities outside the center prevents nuisance and helps people better move forward.

120062.explanation: Accessibility is important. However, smart solutions are needed.

120063.explanation: Sustainability and affordability must go together. Clean engines must have a place on the road.

120064.explanation: Nijmegen must ensure that traffic flow is increased and not further restricted by narrowings.

120065.explanation: Health comes first. In case of poor air quality, protection must be prioritized.

120066.explanation: Quickly creating additional housing helps reduce pressure. However, clear rules and compliance controls are necessary.

120067.explanation: Safety and care for the vulnerable are central. Closing without alternatives solves nothing.

120069.explanation: Festivities and safety must be balanced. No automatic easing of restrictions.

120070.explanation: We will temporarily stop funding cultural centers.

120002.explanation: Volt opposes a binding corrective referendum. Carefully prepared legislation cannot be replaced by a yes/no question. However, Volt finds it important that residents can participate early in the legislative process. Therefore, we support citizens' assemblies where Nijmegen residents can express their opinions in a timely manner.

120005.explanation: Volt supports the idea that cafés and restaurants have flexibility within the General Local Regulation (APV) to set their own opening hours, under certain conditions. It must be in consultation with the municipality and appropriate for the specific location, with attention to nuisance and the living environment.

120009.explanation: A lot of energy escapes through terrace heating, and we must drastically reduce our CO₂ emissions. There are also cheaper and more sustainable options for heating a terrace, such as infrared panels or heated cushions.

120010.explanation: In light of the general fireworks ban at the end of 2026, it is good that the municipality immediately starts creating a new tradition in the form of a fireworks show. This can limit many downsides of fireworks (injuries, violence, damage, and environmental pollution).

120015.explanation: Local media play a crucial role in informing citizens and monitoring local governance. This fits within our pursuit of a strong local democracy.

120020.explanation: Volt supports more public enforcement officers (boa’s) only when they are deployed within an integral safety plan. Local coordination based on clear data and priorities is necessary. Boa’s must collaborate well with police, community workers, and care providers. Feelings of insecurity must be taken seriously.

120021.explanation: Volt is not in favor of large-scale camera surveillance. We are open to strategic, transparent, location-specific camera surveillance in areas with real safety issues. Social control, behavior change, and better design of public spaces are at least as effective as cameras.

120024.explanation: This measure unfortunately only drives up prices. We want more affordable housing, especially for single-person households, and the Affordable Rent Act must be enforced more strictly. It should also become easier to split large homes.

120027.explanation: Volt supports maintaining an owner-occupancy obligation for buyers. This ensures buyers will actually live in Nijmegen. Housing has been left to the market for years. Volt wants to increase the percentage of social rental and affordable housing in new construction projects.

120030.explanation: Instead of a mandatory counter-performance, Volt opts for support towards work with language and training programs. We encourage voluntary engagement alongside an obligation to apply for jobs. We experiment with, among other things, a basic income to give people more space and control over their efforts.

120032.explanation: The income threshold must be raised. This will also allow many low-income workers access to support. Poverty policy needs to be drastically simplified, based on trust.

120034.explanation: Volt wants a system that replaces all allowances and discounts with a monthly basic income. This provides the security you deserve and the freedom to progress. This creates space for solidarity; for example, for informal care without financial worries, and for a life without debt.

120036.explanation: Volt aims for climate neutrality by 2040. Driving 30 kilometers per hour in more areas within built-up zones means more traffic safety, better livability, and less pollution in densely populated areas. This must be locally and appropriately organized per area.

120039.explanation: Volt regards healthcare as a basic right: accessible to everyone. Freedom of choice is a central principle. People should be able to trust their healthcare provider. At the same time, there is a shortage in healthcare. We cannot afford to provide every resident with their preferred healthcare provider.

120045.explanation: Volt advocates for a green, innovative, and circular economy. Expansion must contribute to the economic transition. Spatial decisions must take biodiversity and climate into account. Local support is necessary, involving residents and entrepreneurs in the decision-making process.

120047.explanation: Refugees do not have the chance to build long waiting times. Part of the housing stock must be reserved for status holders. This allows status holders to contribute sooner to society, integrate faster, and reduces costs in shelters by having fewer status holders in reception centers.

120049.explanation: Volt supports generating green electricity and therefore opposes banning solar panels on agricultural land. However, solar panels on roofs have higher priority. A green environment is also important but can be stimulated in various ways, including greening streets and neighborhoods.

120053.explanation: Volt recognizes the Nimwegen dialect as cultural heritage but will not impose anything on primary schools regarding this. Given the overcrowded agendas of primary schools to meet core objectives, this does not seem a priority.

120055.explanation: Volt wants to increase the percentage of social rental homes to 35% in new construction projects to make housing more affordable.

120061.explanation: Volt acknowledges the nuisance around day and night shelters for the homeless in the city center but looks for broader solutions beyond merely relocating them.

120062.explanation: Volt supports a car-free city center, with exceptions for residents and destination traffic. More parking spaces must be created around the city center but not within it.

120063.explanation: Electric mopeds and scooters are cleaner, better for air quality, and cause less noise nuisance. To support the switch to currently often more expensive electric models, the existing subsidy scheme remains intact.

120064.explanation: Volt advocates for a car-free city center. Through traffic should be diverted around the city as much as possible, and there must be sufficient paid parking space at the city edges. Road narrowings around the center must be assessed case-by-case for their contribution.

120065.explanation: Volt strives for a climate-neutral society before 2040, with clean energy and air as key focuses. Wood burning is a major source of fine particulate matter and pollution of clean air, contributing to health complaints and worse air quality during orange and red alerts.

120066.explanation: Splitting homes helps address the housing shortage. Additionally, 40% of households are single-person households. Splitting homes also meets the demand for smaller homes.

120067.explanation: Volt sees sex work as an equal part of the service sector and wants to eliminate stigma against sex workers. It is important that there are good and safe working conditions and supervision against human trafficking.

120069.explanation: The sale of alcohol in supermarkets causes a lot of nuisance, litter, and affects local entrepreneurs. Therefore, Volt maintains the alcohol ban.

120070.explanation: Volt wants everyone to have the chance to enjoy culture. De Lindenberg is an important cultural venue and should be financially supported, but not at any cost.

120002.explanation: Referenda are better than only having a say every four years. Yet, they are poor pseudo-democracy because they simplify complex topics into for or against. Nijmegen residents must not have to fight outside the city council to have their voices heard and should be heard in other ways.

120005.explanation: The current rules suffice for the hospitality industry. Employees and neighborhood residents have a right to peace and quiet.

120009.explanation: Terrace heating simply wastes too much energy. Heated cushions and blankets are a good alternative.

120010.explanation: If we set the right priorities, there is enough money in our city to centrally organize enjoyable things for Nijmegen residents.

120015.explanation: This money is better spent on culture for and by the neighborhoods.

120020.explanation: Safety is not simply more enforcement officers on the street. Safety lies in addressing the root causes of problems. Violence against women stems from misogyny and is not stopped in practice by public enforcement officers. The same applies to crime arising from poverty.

120021.explanation: The government does not need to monitor every moment of the day.

120024.explanation: The municipality must invest this money in building more social housing, for and by Nijmegen.

120027.explanation: Houses are meant to live in, not to speculate on. In the municipality with the highest housing shortage outside the Randstad, it is unjustifiable that houses are used as profit objects instead of living spaces. In addition, we are building social housing massively with a municipal construction company.

120030.explanation: In theory, the RSP supports the right and (to a reasonable extent) duty to perform socially meaningful work, but not at welfare benefit levels-instead at 80% of the modal income.

120032.explanation: Do not wait until problems have become extreme. A large-scale campaign to eradicate poverty is needed. It is unacceptable that children in West are uncertain about the end of the month, while the companies polluting the air they breathe are thriving.

120034.explanation: The RSP finds a basic income less terrible than the existence of poverty. At the same time, the basic income is a failed derivative of the right and duty to work for a decent wage. The RSP wants to eliminate unemployment and thus make a basic income unnecessary.

120036.explanation: For most roads, it is safer and ultimately not much slower if the maximum speed is reduced to 30 km/h. However, the RSP is not so much focused on replacing the speed limit sign as on redesigning the road network to give more space to playing children, pedestrians, etc.

120039.explanation: This sounds noble but in practice fosters segregation and drives costs up. In addition, it creates an opportunity for parents to isolate their children from social norms.

120045.explanation: Nijmegen already has the largest industrial area in a city center and relatively large business parks. And yet there is too little space to build. When it negatively affects livability, business parks will not be further expanded.

120047.explanation: The alternative is that they end up on the street.

120049.explanation: This is probably not a profitable use of land in the Netherlands, but that is no reason to specifically prohibit it.

120053.explanation: Speaking and writing the Nijmegen dialect should not be punished at school, but no time needs to be dedicated to it either.

120055.explanation: Central and well-prepared planning is exactly why Nijmegen is the fastest building municipality, but it is not enough yet. We are also the municipality with the largest housing shortage outside the Randstad. We need a municipal construction company that builds social housing massively for people, not for profit.

120061.explanation: The alternative is that these people sleep on the streets. Shelters must increase in capacity and be free. So that no one has to sleep in the freezing cold. This is life-threatening.

120062.explanation: Driving in the city center is not necessary if you do not live in the center. But resident permits must be free, and parking rental should only be accessible to people who live in the center.

120063.explanation: If you want to get rid of scooters and mopeds in the center, public transport to neighborhoods far from the center must be improved rather than removing mobility options.

120064.explanation: Ultimately, car traffic in the center must be limited to residents, and the immediate surroundings of the center should reasonably be limited as a through route. We acknowledge that Nijmegen is a uniquely difficult case in this regard.

120066.explanation: Splitting houses pushes starters and self-employed people into lower quality homes. A real solution to the housing shortage is a municipal construction company where living, not profit, is the priority.

120067.explanation: The causes of (street) prostitution must be addressed. Inequality, poverty, and homelessness are policy choices. Street prostitution cannot be voted away in a municipal council by pushing it out of sight.

120069.explanation: This measure is largely designed to enrich entrepreneurs at the expense of the partygoers’ freedom of choice.

120070.explanation: Lindenberg is important for Nijmegen’s cultural life. If there is money, it is better spent on Lindenberg than on profiteers and “loans” to failed entrepreneurs.

statements.title: Nijmegen should allow the sale of alcohol in supermarkets again during the Four Days Marches week.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Nijmegen should allow the subdivision of homes much more often.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The day and night shelters for homeless people must be removed from the centre.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The residents of Nijmegen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Nijmegen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Nijmegen should close the tippelzone.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Business parks in Nijmegen should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Nijmegen should run a pilot project with a basic income.

titleParts.text: Nijmegen moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: Nijmegen needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should narrow the roads around the centre.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Nimweegs dialect.
Nijmegen should repeal the ban on the use of non-electric mopeds and scooters as of 2028.
At least 35% of the newly built homes in Nijmegen should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Nijmegen moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
More parking spaces should be provided in and around the centre.
The municipality should contribute generously to the new construction or renovation of Lindenberg Cultuurhuis.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet royaal meebetalen aan nieuwbouw of

titleParts.glossary: The repair or partial renovation of an existing building.

statements.title: More CCTV surveillance should be installed in Nijmegen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Nijmegen should prohibit wood burning when air quality codes are orange and red.
